title: Media, Connectors, Transceivers, and Architectures · Packet Forensics
summary: Select and document a campus and data-center physical design under distance, bandwidth, environment, and redundancy constraints. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a physical media and topology bill of design and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Compare 802.3 copper categories, shielding, distance, speed, duplex, PoE, coaxial, DAC, and twinax use cases.
Compare single-mode and multimode fiber, wavelengths, distance, bandwidth, bend, contamination, and safety concerns.
Compare 802.11, cellular, and satellite media by coverage, latency, capacity, interference, mobility, and cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
Use a changed debug case derived from this module: network media topologies. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-campus-datacenter-architecture-14zac6" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Campus Datacenter Architecture into observable performance evidence.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Campus Datacenter Architecture applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare three-tier, collapsed-core, and spine-leaf architectures plus north-south and east-west traffic.
